title: Retrospective Initial Reactions
wikitext:
[]: Retrospective: Initial Reactions             Quote Post by Shay Guy » Wed Feb 04, 2009 2:34 am I like history. I don't know why, or how long I have. Maybe it grew from my fondness for classic music and movies. Maybe it's just math -- there's a lot more "back then" than there is "right now," so there's a lot more interesting stuff from "back then" than there is "right now." In any event, this interest of mine includes fandom. I have an interest in 1990s fan fiction, for instance, and sometimes I search in the forums for my favorite webcomics to see how its readers reacted when certain strips were posted. Where am I going with all this? Just this: our fandom is over a decade old. Neon Genesis Evangelion first began airing on Japanese television in late 1995, over 13 years ago as of this writing. It may have been the most influential anime series of the 1990s. It was unlike anything that came before, so it is said. What was it like to be an anime fan back then, and to be presented with... this ? This, my friends, is why I love Google Groups. It seems to have been some time before full awareness of Eva filtered into the rec.arts.anime newsgroup. Feels weird from my point of view, living in an age where an episode airs one day, the subs are out the next, and the blogs are all over it the next. In any case, the earliest discussion I could find with Advanced Search is in mid-1995, with a few mentions of the already-running manga and hopes that it'd live up to Gunbuster/Nadia/Honneamise/Otaku no Video. Here's the first review I can find, covering the first episode, but it doesn't go into great detail. This thread goes over the first four episodes in more depth. [code:1] In the year 2000, aliens calling themselves the Disciples attack Earth mercilessly as part of a jihad, causing destruction so massive weather patterns all over the globe were disrupted for decades. They give the people of Earth an ultimatum: we must stop all research on genetic engineering OR ELSE. The Disciples leave, promising they will return to make sure we are obeying. Earth, of course, didn't comply. The United Nations created a covert organization called NERV whose sole purpose is to handle the alien threat when it returns. Taking a cue from the aliens' own gigantic war machines (codenamed "Angels"), a team of scientists working under the leadership of Gendoh Ikari design bionegineered humanoid war-machines, the Evangelions. Our story begins in 2015, as the Angels return to Earth.[/code:1] ...Wha? Without [Misato], I think the show would be too serious.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Feb 04, 2009 3:18 pm ep16 has Yui and Rei both use the 'Well, that's good for you" line, with Shinji being a bit shocked at the parallel. It is rather interesting stuff. keep it up!

[]:             Quote Post by Annihilationscape » Wed Feb 04, 2009 4:35 pm I'm diggin' this. I share a similar historical interest; to see what the speculation was and what sort of supporting evidence used. It's a kind of wistful 'what if' type of thing for me. Keep 'em coming if you get the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Feb 04, 2009 6:14 pm Most of the first generation fans that have dropped by here (most of them still hang out at the EML) started at Japan.anime.evangelion. That's where a lot of the translations and production/concept materials first showed up (like the alternate endings of EoE). It's not english specific but a lot of english speakers are there. Someone put together a faq a while ago from Japan.anime.evangelion posts: http://www.users.on.net/~attema/Pen3/faq/ some of it has also been compiled by MDWigs: http://www.angelfire.com/anime4/mdwigs/ I didn't watch Eva until 2005-ish partly out of avoiding it, but back then I never had problems finding shows and never felt the need to discuss them with people. By the time Eva aired in Japan I'd already graduated from University so I didn't have the benefit of clubs to see what was popular in the states, although I did have a friend in Japan that regularly mailed me VHS tapes of whatever he thought was good.
